documents.cleaned_text: BOROUGH OF BELMAR PLANNING BOARD NOTICE OF PLANNING BOARD MEETING CONCERNING THE AMENDED HOUSING ELEMENT AND FAIR SHARE PLAN OF THE BOROUGH’S MASTER PLAN PLEASE TAKE NOTICE that on February 26, 2026 at 7:00 p.m., in accordance with the Municipal Land Use Law, more particularly N.J.S.A. 40:55D-13, the Planning Board of the Borough of Belmar, County of Monmouth, State of New Jersey, at its regular meeting, will conduct a public hearing and take action on the Amended Housing Element and Fair Share Plan (“HEFSP”) of the Borough’s Master Plan, dated February, 2026, in connection with a proceeding known as, In the Matter of the Application of the Borough of Belmar Municipal Corporation of the State of New Jersey Declaratory Judgment Action, bearing Docket No. MON-L-655-23, pending in the Superior Court before the Honorable Linda Grasso Jones, J.S.C., Law Division, Monmouth County (the “DJ Action”). This proceeding involves litigation filed by the Borough of Belmar pursuant to the New Jersey Supreme Court’s March 10, 2015 decision in the matter of In re N.J.A.C. 5:96 & N.J.A.C. 5:97, 221 N.J. 1 (2015). As part of the settlement and resolution of the DJ Action, the Borough’s Planning Consultant, Leon S. Avakian Inc., (the “Planning Consultant”) prepared the Housing Element and Fair Share Plan (the “Original HEFSP”), an element of the Borough’s Master Plan, in which it identified specific sites and compliance mechanisms to address the Borough’s housing obligations that present a realistic opportunity for the development of housing affordable to very low, low and moderate income households. The Original HEFSP also delineates the actions taken, and to be taken, by the Borough to satisfy its Fourth Round affordable housing responsibilities under applicable laws. The Borough’s Fourth Round affordable housing obligation was determined by the New Jersey Department of Community Affairs to be a present need or rehabilitation component of 0 affordable units and a prospective need or new construction obligation of 43 units. The Borough’s Realistic Development Potential is 14 as determined pursuant to the Fair Housing Act, N.J.S.A. 52:27D-301, et seq., the substantive, applicable regulations, the New Jersey Supreme Court’s March 10, 2015 decision in the matter of In re N.J.A.C. 5:96 & N.J.A.C. 5:97, 221 N.J. 1 (2015), and other applicable laws. The Borough Planning Board adopted the Original HEFSP on June 16, 2025. Following subsequent negotiations with Fair Share Housing Center, as part of the settlement and resolution of the DJ Action, the Planning Consultant prepared an amendment to the Original HEFSP (the “Amended HEFSP”). Following public comment, the Planning Board shall consider adoption of the Amended HEFSP by way of resolution. The Amended HEFSP was prepared in accordance with N.J.S.A. 40:55D-28(b)(3) of the MLUL and the mandatory requirements of a Housing Element under the New Jersey Fair Housing Act, N.J.S.A. 52:27D-301 to -329.20, and the Administrative Office of the Courts Directive #14-24. A copy of the Amended HEFSP, and any other relevant documents, are on file and available for public inspection and/or photocopying (at requestor’s expense) during normal business hours, beginning on February 12, 2026, in the Borough Clerk’s office located at 601 Main 675521.1 Street, Belmar, New Jersey 07719 and is posted on the Borough’s website. Any interested person may appear and be heard at the time of the Planning Board Meeting on February 26, 2026, to discuss and offer comments on the HEFSP in accordance with the rules of the Planning Board.

All planning/zoning board application fees and escrow deposits must be paid by checks payable to “Borough of Belmar” and delivered to the Board Secretary, Borough of Belmar Municipal Building, Belmar Municipal Building 601 Main Street, Belmar, NJ 07719.** **Anyone who receives a request purporting to require a wire transfer or other electronic payment to the Borough of Belmar should immediately contact the Board Secretary by phone at 732-681-3700 ext. 225 , because it is a scam.** No item found! # General Information No item found! The Board of Adjustment is responsible for reviewing variance applications, hearing appeals of the decisions of the zoning officer, and ruling on such other matters as the law allows. Property owners within 200 feet of properties that are the subject of applications before the Board of Adjustment are notified of the application. All plans and application documents are on file with the Board Secretary and are available for inspection and review during normal business hours at least 10 days prior to the hearing on the application. All taxpayers, residents and other interested parties are welcome to voice opinions, concerns or support during the public comment portion of the hearing on the application. **For questions please contact the secretary at 732-681-3700 ext. 225.** **Who We Are** The Board of Adjustment consists of 7 members and 2-4 alternates. The Board of Adjustment Attorney is Kevin Kennedy, Esq. The Board of Adjustment Secretary is April Claudio. **Meetings** The Board of Adjustment meets on the 4th Thursday of every month unless otherwise noted. # Zoning Board

### Who We Are

**Kevin E. Kennedy, Esq**  
Zoning Board Attorney

**April Claudio**  
Zoning Board Secretary

###### Upcoming Zoning Board Meetings

23  Apr

[Zoning Board of Adjustment Meeting](/resident/calendar/zoning-board-of-adjustment-meeting-2-1776981600)

04.23.2026 6:00 pm

28  May

[Zoning Board of Adjustment Meeting](/resident/calendar/zoning-board-of-adjustment-meeting-2-1780005600)

05.28.2026 6:00 pm

25  Jun

[Zoning Board of Adjustment Meeting](/resident/calendar/zoning-board-of-adjustment-meeting-2-1782424800)

06.25.2026 6:00 pm

23  Jul

[Zoning Board of Adjustment Meeting](/resident/calendar/zoning-board-of-adjustment-meeting-2-1784844000)

07.23.2026 6:00 pm

27  Aug

[Zoning Board of Adjustment Meeting](/resident/calendar/zoning-board-of-adjustment-meeting-2-1787868000)

08.27.2026 6:00 pm

[Meeting Documents](/government/meeting-documents-2/board-of-adjustment-meetings)

[Zoning Forms & Documents](/government/forms/zoning-department)

**Notice: The Borough of Belmar does not accept wire transfers or other electronic payments.  All planning/zoning board application fees and escrow deposits must be paid by checks payable to “Borough of Belmar” and delivered to the Board Secretary, Borough of Belmar Municipal Building, Belmar Municipal Building 601 Main Street, Belmar, NJ 07719.**

**Anyone who receives a request purporting to require a wire transfer or other electronic payment to the Borough of Belmar should immediately contact the Board Secretary by phone at 732-681-3700 ext. 225 , because it is a scam.**

No item found!

# General Information

No item found!

The Board of Adjustment is responsible for reviewing variance applications, hearing appeals of the decisions of the zoning officer, and ruling on such other matters as the law allows. Property owners within 200 feet of properties that are the subject of applications before the Board of Adjustment are notified of the application. All plans and application documents are on file with the Board Secretary and are available for inspection and review during normal business hours at least 10 days prior to the hearing on the application.

All taxpayers, residents and other interested parties are welcome to voice opinions, concerns or support during the public comment portion of the hearing on the application.

**For questions please contact the secretary at 732-681-3700 ext. 225.**

**Who We Are**  
The Board of Adjustment consists of 7 members and 2-4 alternates.  
The Board of Adjustment Attorney is Kevin Kennedy, Esq.  
The Board of Adjustment Secretary is April Claudio.

**Meetings**  
The Board of Adjustment meets on the 4th Thursday of every month unless otherwise noted. Meetings begin at 6:00 PM in the courtroom in the municipal building.

![Belmar Municipal]()
![Belmar Municipal]()

**P: 732-681-3700**  
**F: 732-681-3434**

**General Inquiries**  
[**info@belmar.com**](mailto:info@belmar.com)

**Belmar Municipal Building**  
**601 Main Street, Belmar, NJ 07719**

![Belmar Municipal]()
![Belmar Municipal]()

**P: 732-681-3700**  
**F: 732-681-3434**

**General Inquiries**  
[**info@belmar.com**](mailto:info@belmar.com)

**Belmar Municipal Building**  
**601 Main Street, Belmar, NJ 07719**

# Planning Board

### Who We Are

**The Planning Board consists of 9 members and 2 alternates.**

**Douglas Kovats, Esq.**  
Planning Board Attorney

**April Claudio**  
Planning Board Secretary

No item found!

# General Information

No item found!

The Planning Board hears applications for sub-divisions, site plans, as well as applications for the Seaport Redevelopment and Transit Village. Homeowners within 200 feet of properties being reviewed by the Planning Board are notified of an application and are welcome to voice opinions, concerns or support during the public comment portion of the meeting. The Planning Board is also responsible for the Belmar Master Plan.

Any questions please call 732-681-3700 ext. 225

**Meetings**  
The Planning Board meets on the 3rd Monday of every month unless otherwise noted. Meetings begin at 7:00 pm and are held in the courtroom in the municipal building.

###### Upcoming Planning Board Meetings

**Notice: The Borough of Belmar does not accept wire transfers or other electronic payments.  All planning/zoning board application fees and escrow deposits must be paid by checks payable to “Borough of Belmar” and delivered to the Board Secretary, Borough of Belmar Municipal Building, Belmar Municipal Building 601 Main Street, Belmar, NJ 07719.**

**Anyone who receives a request purporting to require a wire transfer or other electronic payment to the Borough of Belmar should immediately contact the Board Secretary by phone at 732-681-3700 ext. 225 , because it is a scam.**
